1.description: An example of a typical leveraged fund. The figure illustrates a typical leveraged fund using the official webpage for ProShares Ultra Financials. Panel A shows the fund overview and Panel B shows the fund holdings. The rows after the stock holding of JPMORGAN CHASE & CO are not shown to save space. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Cost of leverage and the shadow cost of leverage constraints. Panel A shows the three-month moving average of our cost of leverage measure (rc), as defined in equation (10), as well as the three-month moving averages of the benchmark risk-free rates: the four-week Treasury bill rate (T-bill 1M) and the general collateral repo rate (GC repo). Panel B plots the three-month moving averages of our shadow cost measure (ψ, the difference betweenrcand the GC repo rate) and the TED spread. The light blue shaded areas indicate the U.S. recession from December 2007 to June 2009 as defined by National Bureau of Economic Research (NBER), and the gray shaded areas indicate the Euro area recession from October 2011 to March 2013 as defined by Center for Economic Policy Research (CEPR). [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: Cumulative alpha. This figure plots the cumulative alphas for the univariateβψ1−5portfolio with respect to the CAPM, FF3, and FF5 models from July 2007 to December 2016. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
